# [Do we have a Democracy?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=180547444&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwNTQ3NDQ0LCJpYXQiOjE3NjQ3MTYzOTMsImV4cCI6MTc2NzMwODM5MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.lwASN0Y1DuQK4IOl_6NVS6Da8cjx_WrIzFkAjR3XTqg)
### Where do we go from here?
| | [Lucas Kunce](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---
| Dec 2
---
| [](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=180547444&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwNTQ3NDQ0LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NDcxNjM5MywiZXhwIjoxNzY3MzA4MzkzLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTg2Nzc0MSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.UdolbMXlNieVg8X16xpvkuYOT5r-dowQzOUzN6ZcZ50&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=180547444&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwNTQ3NDQ0LCJpYXQiOjE3NjQ3MTYzOTMsImV4cCI6MTc2NzMwODM5MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.lwASN0Y1DuQK4IOl_6NVS6Da8cjx_WrIzFkAjR3XTqg&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=180547444&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgwNTQ3NDQ0LCJpYXQiOjE3NjQ3MTYzOTMsImV4cCI6MTc2NzMwODM5MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.lwASN0Y1DuQK4IOl_6NVS6Da8cjx_WrIzFkAjR3XTqg)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvbHVjYXNrdW5jZS9wL2RvLXdlLWhhdmUtYS1kZW1vY3JhY3k_.yB1D4EXZQKtdkRvCaDKQgV5LQn8Adfla8hQfgqGrVfM?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/lucaskunce/p/do-we-have-a-democracy?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Throughout my senate campaign last year, one of the biggest motivators for Democrats was that they felt American democracy was at stake in the 2024 election. Since then, that feeling has exploded, with No Kings protests and a full spectrum of dialogue on how the current administration is an existential threat to democracy.
Yet, on the flip side, I discovered during that same campaign that many Missourians outside the Democratic Party felt like electing Donald Trump was the single most democratic choice they had made in their lifetimes.
How can that be, and what can we learn from it?
Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.f5I144UEoSIwdA-2KBCOBNtqhb6tslI1Kwn6t_zMlck?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=180547444)
At the root of this difference is a long growing frustration among Americans that our democracy is just an illusion. Many people, of all walks of life, believe that the Democrat and Republican parties of the last couple of decades had essentially merged into a "uni-party" whose primary objective was to those who have money, access, and power with little regard for how the rest of us were doing. Sure, the politicians fought about things on the margins, but they always seemed to agree on who should have power in this country: the "elites." Academics, financiers, lobbyists, large corporations, big donors, etc.
This belief is strongly reflected in polling and surveys.
[70% of Americans](https://substack.com/redirect/73541db2-1b24-4177-9076-c1a10ea30881?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), for example, believe that the people who live in a representative's district have too little influence over the decisions their representatives make. An even larger percentage believe that lobbyists, special interests, and large campaign donors have too much influence. I suspect many of you are among that majority.
And Americans think the barriers to changing this are insurmountable; a full 85% of agree that the cost of political campaigns make it hard for good people to run for office.
It's not just a feeling, either. It's backed up by fact. In 2024, [94% of federal house races were won by the person who spent the most money](https://substack.com/redirect/7f547aff-7e0c-4172-a430-af8e6d56a8c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). My personal experience bears that out. In each election I was a part of, both primary and general, the winner was the one who had the most money (thank you to everyone who supported and made sure I was in that column at least once!).
Money, special interests, and lobbyists are a huge reason people think American democracy is an illusion, but the doubts don't end there. Access to higher office has been gatekept for generations not just by campaign donors, but also by a small group of political insiders, in many cases, even the same families.
Our country is beset by political dynasties from the bottom all the way to the top.
Was George W. Bush really the most meritorious person in our country to be nominated by the Republican Party and then be President? Was Hillary Clinton the best nominee in 2016 for that matter, or was she also just part of the right family? How are there always Kennedy's running around in multiple offices regardless of their talent-level or scandals? Would people be [talking about a Trump kid](https://substack.com/redirect/ce4172ae-51ab-4629-8141-93a5cb3c0fc4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or [Michelle Obama running for President](https://substack.com/redirect/f38a94f3-bcea-45b1-9e87-4cfa1883638a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) if not for their next of kin?
I don't think so.
Crazy stat: There are more than 700 families in which two or more family members have served in Congress. [In 1978, the most recent statistic I could find, ](https://substack.com/redirect/dc98dc9c-f790-43e5-a3c4-475e852e915e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)these 700 families accounted for nearly 20% of the 10,000 total men and women who had ever served in Congress.
That's mind-blowing.
And it's at every level of our government. Every state and even municipality has multiple political families. The guy running for State Senate in my hometown? Son of the previous state senator. In St. Louis, political family members casually pass statehouse seats off to one another. If you walk through the halls of the Missouri Capitol and look at the classes of Senators and Representatives you'll find the same names over and over and over and over again.
It's pervasive in both parties. It _feels_ anti-democratic and people are finally tired of it.
Just a couple of weeks ago, Congressman Chuy Garcia of Chicago gave us a classic example by literally hand-selecting his own Chief of Staff to serve as his successor in Congress by quietly withdrawing his re-election filing on the last day of the filing period, making sure that his Chief of Staff was the only person who knew he was retiring and who would be able to file before the deadline ended. He didn't even hide it - he was literally the first person to sign her signature petition document days before he announced his retirement!
As far as a lot of folks can tell, the parties, the entrenched politicians, and the donor class pick candidates for the rest of us to choose between and make sure that, regardless of who we pick, it's going to be someone who serves the "uni-party" interests. Normal people have no path to real representation and will never have access to the levers of power.
Ask them who they think is in charge in this country, and who is making the decisions, and essentially no one is going to say "the people."
With that background in place, we come to the intoxicatingly empowering feeling many got by voting for Donald Trump.
Voting for him was the first time a disenfranchised voter felt like they could stick it to the "uni-party" and claw some power back for themselves. Because voting for Donald Trump, and Donald Trump winning, was a voter-led rejection of every single insider from both parties who were absolutely against him becoming president.
Not only that, but it felt even more democratic to vote for him in 2016 and 2024, the two times Trump won, because of how _un_ democratic the selection process for his opponent seemed, with the people in charge literally deciding what option voters would have and telling them how it was going to be.
In 2016, the Democratic superdelegate machine and others lined up behind Hillary Clinton and forced her nomination through to the finish line. In 2024, there wasn't even a primary(!), and by the time Joe Biden decided not to run the only candidate who could take his place was Kamala Harris, a 2020 presidential hopeful who had been forced to drop out of that race before any primary or caucus voting even took place because she had so little support.
The Democratic candidates were machine picks running against a man that the machine hated.
I know a lot of people reading this don't want to believe it, but electing Donald Trump was the first time that many Americans felt true democracy had been fulfilled in our country in their lifetimes. For them, it was a win over a broken system that had cast aside the will of the people for way too long. A feeling that made it natural for them to accept that the 2020 election was rigged. Of course the same people who hated Trump and had spent decades systematically subverting American democracy and neglecting the needs of the American people would rig an election to protect a rigged system. They had been rigging elections with money, power, and gatekeeping for as long as anyone could remember.
I heard all of this and more traveling the state of Missouri for four years over the course of two campaigns.
And I believe it is a massive mistake to ignore it or think it's stupid. It is a strong signal that there is a massive group of voters who will vote to buck the broken system, regardless of party (the mythical Obama/Trump voter was likely doing that by voting for Obama in 2008 and again by voting for Trump in 2016).
And if a party can capture that aura going forward - by rejecting certain types of money, by running fresh faces and ideas, by saying things that the machine can't stand - there is a massive victory there for the taking. The type of victory Democrats secured in the wake of the 1920s.
But right now, I don't think there's any way to tell where those voters are going. I would say now, more than ever, particularly after the chaos of this last year and the dragging economy, that they are open to breaking in the direction of either party, if someone captures that spirit.
But capturing that spirit is critical. It's not going to come on the back of "protecting the ACA" or "returning to normalcy" or any of those half-measure talking points that don't really mean anything. It's going to take actions that look like real sacrifice- changes and positions that hurt. That's how a party becomes believable and gains the trust of a very wary group of voters. Big moves like changes in leadership or changes in funding. Or truly bold ideas or bold rejections of past positions that seem comfortable for politicians but that have failed the people.
Whoever is willing to make those moves is going to be in the position to capture those votes.
If neither party tries, then who knows. The pendulum might swing back to Democrats, [although the party's favorability is still hovering near its decades-record low](https://substack.com/redirect/c2489620-e6de-4d8d-83a0-731e9da22611?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Or it might not.
Lucas

President Donald Trump **[called Somali migrants to Minnesota"garbage"](https://substack.com/redirect/6f15bdad-6341-4605-9178-272d70a54cf7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** who rely too heavily on welfare during his ninth Cabinet meeting of 2025, characterizing Rep. Ilhan Omar as their political figurehead, as a New York Times report claimed ICE is preparing an "intensive immigration enforcement operation" targeting the Twin Cities--though DHS officials said the agency never prosecutes based on race, only immigration status.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/6f15bdad-6341-4605-9178-272d70a54cf7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **President criticizes Walz over fraud schemes:** Trump lambasted Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz as "grossly incompetent" over a burgeoning scandal involving what the Times described as "several fraud schemes proliferated in parts of Minnesota's Somali community," where multiple individuals **[allegedly created companies that billed the state for millions in fraudulent payments,](https://substack.com/redirect/67f1b475-a5dc-4d7d-8dea-6f839b7c84ea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with Walz defending that "criminals find the loopholes" in programs designed to help people.
* **Omar fires back at Trump:** Trump revisited allegations that Omar "married her brother" to obtain U.S. citizenship--claims she has called "absurd and offensive"--and said "when they come from hell, and they complain and do nothing but bitch -- we don't want them in our country," prompting Omar to respond that the president's "obsession with me is creepy" and that "I hope he gets the help he desperately needs."

# **RFK Jr cancer diagnosis?**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/aa77aa20-aff4-49ad-9cc8-d6c7a0c32c0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Dear Reader,
After countless brain scans...
And appointments with some of America's top doctors...
RFK Jr received the worst news of his life... "it's brain cancer".
**[What happened next](https://substack.com/redirect/aa77aa20-aff4-49ad-9cc8-d6c7a0c32c0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** is the subject of great debate... And what RFK Jr is about to do could just save your life.
**[Click here now](https://substack.com/redirect/aa77aa20-aff4-49ad-9cc8-d6c7a0c32c0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for the shocking truth.
P.S. Liberals have been laughing about this but RFK Jr's bravery in the face of a medical death sentence is nothing short of heroic. To learn what happened and why RFK Jr's response could save millions of seniors, **[watch this now.](https://substack.com/redirect/aa77aa20-aff4-49ad-9cc8-d6c7a0c32c0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/aa77aa20-aff4-49ad-9cc8-d6c7a0c32c0c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# **Hawley revives bill demanding stricter vetting for Afghan evacuees after DC shooting**
Senator Josh Hawley is bringing back legislation **[requiring enhanced screening procedures for Afghan evacuees](https://substack.com/redirect/5c41d12a-d113-4b45-824a-931821d89a1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** admitted to the United States during the chaotic 2021 military withdrawal from Afghanistan.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5c41d12a-d113-4b45-824a-931821d89a1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Comprehensive vetting requirements:** The Afghanistan Vetting and Accountability Act would mandate the Department of Homeland Security verify biometric and personal data for all Afghan evacuees, conduct face-to-face screening interviews, suspend federal assistance to those who haven't completed vetting, and deliver quarterly congressional progress reports on the process.
* **Deadly catalyst for action:** The renewed push follows last week's shooting of two National Guard members in Washington by Rahmanullah Lakanwal, a 29-year-old Afghan national admitted under Biden's Operation Allies Welcome program, which left Sarah Beckstrom dead and Andrew Wolfe fighting for his life.
* **Pattern of security concerns:** Another Afghan evacuee from the same resettlement initiative, Mohammad Dawood Alokozay, was arrested the same week after posting TikTok footage suggesting he was constructing an explosive device targeting the Fort Worth region, reinforcing critics' arguments that thousands entered without adequate security checks.

Hawley accused Biden of endangering Americans by allowing massive numbers of refugees into the country without proper screening, stating his legislation will formalize Trump's approach to protecting citizens from potential threats.
* * *
# **NYU student assaulted by stranger on way to class: "Reflect on who you're voting for!"**
An NYU student was **[violently assaulted](https://substack.com/redirect/b2448b73-c919-4232-81e7-89790702104b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** while walking to her morning class in Lower Manhattan, with surveillance footage capturing the disturbing incident that has left the young woman shaken.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b2448b73-c919-4232-81e7-89790702104b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Unprovoked assault details:** Amelia Lewis was walking down Broadway listening to music around 9:30 a.m. Monday when an assailant struck her from behind, grabbed her by the hair, and threw her to the ground before fleeing down Waverly while shocked bystanders rushed to help, with video showing the attacker stalking her before the sudden violence.
* **Suspect in custody:** Authorities have a person of interest facing forcible touching charges along with other offenses, described by Lewis as a tall man with brunette hair and beard wearing grey sweatpants, a large black fur jacket, and a blue towel around his neck.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/d8d35c8b-39a3-4307-b3a4-bf170cc950a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Student demands action:** Lewis posted tearful TikTok and X videos expressing outrage that such incidents can occur in the city, declaring she shouldn't fear walking to her 9:30 a.m. class and urging voters to reconsider their support for current leadership, stating "New York needs help and we're just not getting the help we need."

* * *
# **Noem blasts Biden for using DHS to "invade the country with terrorists"**
Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem unleashed fierce criticism of former President Joe Biden's immigration policies, accusing him of **[deliberately opening America's borders](https://substack.com/redirect/82b71498-716d-4351-b9b8-58be90e27148?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to dangerous individuals and vowing aggressive deportation operations.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/82b71498-716d-4351-b9b8-58be90e27148?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Scathing Biden indictment:** Noem charged that Biden weaponized the Department of Homeland Security to facilitate an invasion by opening borders, transporting arrivals on airplanes, and inviting anyone who reached U.S. shores into the country, declaring "it's our job to get 'em out" while promising to "send more home for the holidays" so deportees can reunite with families in their home nations.
* **Cabinet coordination efforts:** The DHS chief credited Secretary of State Marco Rubio with negotiating travel documents and third-country arrangements for deportees, Attorney General Pam Bondi with defending enforcement operations against legal challenges from liberal states, and War Secretary Pete Hegseth with destroying narcoterrorist vessels in the Caribbean, while announcing the hiring of 10,000 new ICE officers since Trump took office.
* **Minnesota fraud investigation:** Noem targeted Governor Tim Walz as either "an idiot or he did it on purpose--and I think he's both," alleging Minnesota programs illegally brought in immigrants who fraudulently claimed relationships, submitted fake visa applications, and extracted "hundreds of billions of dollars from taxpayers," with DHS now working to remove those individuals and recover the funds.

Walz responded to The New York Times that state programs are designed to help people, with criminals exploiting loopholes in the system.
* * *
# **White House claps back at Sabrina Carpenter over ICE video**
The White House issued a sharp rebuke to **[singer Sabrina Carpenter](https://substack.com/redirect/1a3d0c7f-3817-4f33-af16-84262ccdade2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/1a3d0c7f-3817-4f33-af16-84262ccdade2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)following her condemnation of an Immigration and Customs Enforcement video that featured her song without authorization.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/99dfc2f2-c0a6-4fed-85aa-4655a8af9252?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Singer 's objection:** Carpenter denounced the ICE deportation footage set to her track "Juno" as reprehensible, insisting her work never be connected to what she termed an inhumane policy agenda.
* **Pointed administration comeback:** White House spokesperson Abigail Jackson responded with a play on Carpenter's album title, refusing to apologize for removing violent criminals from the country and questioning whether those defending such individuals were foolish or simply slow to understand the issue.
* **Split public opinion:** Supporters championed Carpenter as part of a bold new generation of artists willing to take public stands on controversial matters, while detractors argued the government's duty is protecting American citizens first and pointed to past disputes over her provocative content as contradictory to her moral stance.

The contentious exchange centered on footage showing enforcement operations accompanied by Carpenter's music and a caption twisting her song lyrics into a deportation message.
* * *

I’m respectfully asking: [Can you split a donation of $10 to my reelection campaign and the Democratic Party of Georgia today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120472951/586389702/-1748950076?amount=10&source=SBD_DPG_EM_FR_120325_OSSOFFSPLIT_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5YjgxNDAwOS1kNGNmLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qrNMSExwNcHbfAFWEaea32oXQ9Lpehow-Jtgp6n0pt0=) Sorry to be so direct, but winning in 2026 is our only hope to regain Congressional power and reverse our ongoing descent toward one-man rule. And I am the _only_ Democratic U.S. Senator up for reelection in a state Trump won. That makes me MAGA’s #1 target in 2026. They’re already spending MILLIONS of dollars to try to defeat me. **_There is no path back from the brink of authoritarianism without defending this seat — the very seat that secured the Democratic majority in 2021._** So I'm asking for your help. Not for me, **for all of us.** [If you’re able, please make a contribution of any amount to my campaign and the Democratic Party of Georgia today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120472975/586389716/-1748950076?amount=10&source=SBD_DPG_EM_FR_120325_OSSOFFSPLIT_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5YjgxNDAwOS1kNGNmLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qrNMSExwNcHbfAFWEaea32oXQ9Lpehow-Jtgp6n0pt0=) The campaign will be brutal. _But this team wins the hardest battles._ We flipped Georgia when no one thought it was possible. Remember? We unseated an entrenched GOP incumbent to win the Senate majority. And we will win again — with your help. | Georgia Democrats have a long history of fighting for change.

I’m respectfully asking: = Sorry to be so direct, but winning in 2026 is our only hope to regain Congressional power and reverse our ongoing descent toward one-man rule. There is no path back from the brink of authoritarianism without defending this seat — the very seat that secured the Democratic majority in 2021. So I'm asking for your help. Not for me, for all of us. = The campaign will be brutal. But this team wins the hardest battles. We flipped Georgia when no one thought it was possible. Remember? Hi there, While Trump and his allies attack, Arizona Democrats are fighting back — and leading the national resistance. [**Will you chip in to support our work across the state?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120473221/586390138/541746778?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251202_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzg4MGFlNzAtZDRjZi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=WrCUwe6V-aGq_Bg3NzACyTkj4ojSgmCQZ3xcNOorqVE=) | [CHIP IN NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120473222/586390139/79744751?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251202_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzg4MGFlNzAtZDRjZi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=WrCUwe6V-aGq_Bg3NzACyTkj4ojSgmCQZ3xcNOorqVE=)
---
Here are just a few examples of how Arizona Democrats are standing up to Trump in Washington:
* **Senator Mark Kelly** made a video reminding service members they can refuse illegal orders. Trump called him a "traitor" and said he should be "hanged." His response? "I won't be silenced by President Trump's attempt to intimidate me."
* **Senator Ruben Gallego** is focusing our party's message on what working families actually need: affordable healthcare, protection from SNAP benefit cuts, and relief from the inflation crisis Trump's policies are making worse.
* **Representative Adelita Grijalva** stood strong in the face of over a month’s delay to her taking her seat, so that she could be the deciding vote on releasing the Epstein files and hold powerful interests accountable.

This is what leadership looks like. When Arizona Republicans call Democrats "domestic terrorists," lie about healthcare, and bow to Trump's every whim, we stand up for the rule of law, defend working families, and refuse to be intimidated.
**But we can't do it alone.**
By supporting the Arizona Democratic Party, you make it possible for us to build the infrastructure and support the candidates who will stand up to the MAGA agenda in every corner of the state.
We're working to organize in all 15 counties. We're defending statewide offices won by razor-thin margins. We're competing for congressional seats that could flip the House. And we're supporting leaders who won't back down when Trump threatens them.
[**Can you chip in today to support our resistance movement?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120473223/586390140/541746778?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251202_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzg4MGFlNzAtZDRjZi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=WrCUwe6V-aGq_Bg3NzACyTkj4ojSgmCQZ3xcNOorqVE=)
[CHIP IN NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120473224/586390141/79744751?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251202_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzg4MGFlNzAtZDRjZi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=WrCUwe6V-aGq_Bg3NzACyTkj4ojSgmCQZ3xcNOorqVE=)
---
**Arizona is the battleground. Our Democrats are on the front lines. And they need our support.**
Thanks for standing with us,
Arizona Democratic Party

clean_body: If everyone reading this email right now gave just $1, it would push us over the finish line. | Hey Peter, It's Christen Sparago, chief digital officer at EMILYs List. You're probably getting a lot of Giving Tuesday emails, so I wanted to personally reach out to make sure you saw the message we sent you earlier. This opportunity doesn't come around often: Today only, every donation we get before midnight will be MATCHED up to $25,000, for a total Giving Tuesday goal of $50,000 to support our women. And here's some good news: as of 6:30 p.m. ET, we're already 88% of the way there, which means we need to raise just $2,787 before midnight. If everyone reading this email right now gave just $1, it would push us over the finish line and unlock the FULL $50,000 Giving Tuesday match. I'm confident that, with your support, we can finish Giving Tuesday strong and set our women up for success as we head into the midterms. If you've been thinking about giving, now is truly the moment. If you've saved your payment information with FastAction, your donation will go through immediately: | | ---|--- | | See you online, Christen Sparago (they/she) Chief Digital Officer, EMILYs List —Original Message— Sent: Tues., Dec. 2, 2025 From: Callie Fines, EMILYs List Peter, Today is Giving Tuesday — a day defined by generosity, community, and the belief that when we come together, we can change lives. It's a moment, in the thick of the holiday season, to give back, to support the causes that shape our future, and to honor the power we have when we lead with hope. And today, we have the chance to make that impact even greater. In honor of Giving Tuesday, a generous group of grassroots donors has agreed to match every gift that comes in today up to $25,000 — for a total Giving Tuesday goal of $50,000. That means your $10 becomes $20. Your $25 becomes $50. Your $50 becomes $100 — all going directly toward electing Democratic pro-choice women who are fighting for our freedoms. If you've saved your payment information with FastAction, your donation will go through immediately: | ---|--- | | This match is a reflection of what Giving Tuesday is all about: ordinary people stepping up together to do something extraordinary. Every gift today carries twice the power and twice the possibility. As we look toward the critical elections ahead in 2026, your support today could be the difference between winning and losing the races that will determine the future of reproductive freedom in this country. This is our chance to start strong — together — and fuel the leaders who are standing up for our rights, our families, and our democracy. If you’re able, I hope you'll join us and take advantage of this special moment. If you've saved your payment information with FastAction, your donation will go through immediately: | ---|--- | | Thank you for being part of this community — today and every day. With gratitude, Callie Fines (she/her/hers) Chief Financial Officer, EMILYs List = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. Feel free to drop us a line at mailto:information@emilyslist.org or simply reply to this email. Contributions or gifts to EMILYs List or endorsed candidates are not tax deductible. EMILYs List P.O. Box 96612 Washington, DC 20077 United States | ---|---

name: Team Taylor
subject: ICYMI
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Taylor for Justice
body:
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20251202-876&t=1&akid=878%2E294397%2E-_xQ4p)
ICYMI: Yesterday, nomination papers officially dropped, and our campaign is moving full steam ahead.
This is the first major step toward getting Judge Chris Taylor on the ballot and protecting Wisconsin’s progressive majority on the Supreme Court. And it’s going to take all of us working together to organize across every corner of the state.
Right-wing special interests are already lining up behind Maria Lazar -- an extreme candidate who has a track record of opposing reproductive freedom, empowering election deniers, and siding with the far-right.
**They have virtually unlimited money to prop up Maria Lazar.** We don’t have that luxury -- and that’s why we’re working around the clock to compete.
[**Your $10 contribution gives us the grassroots fuel to officially nominate Judge Chris Taylor and overcome whatever the far-right mega donors throw at us. Can we count on your support?**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251202_1_ak.If4bU5&amount=10&refcode=em20251202-876&t=3&akid=878%2E294397%2E-_xQ4p)

Team Taylor

name: Powered by People
subject: Dec 9th—are you in?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
#
Have you heard? Beto is hosting our [**All Vol Call on Tuesday, December 9th,**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/67413?source=ema20251202-240498&t=2&akid=240498%2E444211%2EHinYrm) and he wants you there with us.
At Powered by People, we know organizing works—and we believe Texans can change what’s possible when we show up. **This call is for people who are ready to roll up their sleeves and dive in to talk to voters, build community, and do the work to win up and down the ballot in 2026.**
On December 9th, we’ll share how we’re reaching the decisive voters who will determine the closest races in our state. You’ll hear how our 2024 program outperformed young voter turnout statewide and nationally—not because of luck, but because volunteers _like you_ put in the time, the care, and the conversations that moved people to the polls.
And we’re not stopping there. We’ll walk through exactly how you can get plugged into meaningful, immediate volunteer opportunities—the same work that will help us compete statewide to win Congressional districts, a U.S. Senate seat, and the Governor’s office.
[**If you’re ready to step up and be part of a community that’s building real power in the most voter-suppressed state in the country, RSVP for the call today.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/67413?source=ema20251202-240498&t=3&akid=240498%2E444211%2EHinYrm)
**[RSVP TODAY](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/67413?source=ema20251202-240498&t=4&akid=240498%2E444211%2EHinYrm)**
Texas needs you, and we’d be grateful to have you on the team.
Thank you,
Powered by People

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pbp-em-2023-footer?refcode=em20251202-240498-footer&t=5&refcode2=240498_444211_HinYrm&akid=240498%2E444211%2EHinYrm)**
Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople.org](http://www.poweredxpeople.org?akid=240498%2E444211%2EHinYrm), not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We are powered by grassroots supporters like you. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=6&akid=240498%2E444211%2EHinYrm).

body:
Help us catch up to fight back and protect this seat!

While Washington remains gridlocked, Dr. Raul Ruiz is committed to serving and helping constituents with the problems they have with the federal government and cutting red tape. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/dAfRehWDrkq5GdVTndjWgA2?unsubscribedata=rzZ6q2RAx%2BhhXlGoRAz4DuVaxE%2BFicmIygdc3FJ1b7GRZB4qyRp4Gr6Q0Pa%2FxWzvXFTyWLYBaXTj6xNdDVtc6A3UAo3nu7MplSdRAxUuSpYjWgWZtPRYMSiaA8bH2oyBMqE2wZgMgummkqVYHr0GG6pa9x8vPf1vGNJhVfkISM3TWzDyzFEYipRno%2Fo19SGzxtBGqJh119m4ydxTZZoKapWB%2FWEz6v3mHvMKfbdAfowJ8VEjfWWbg8Nk3YVouhBTjl1GacWZrsyWBmxGt9yAVYfWiv%2FBias5A3gi1CNzp6eB4G%2B%2F3fXgNFbRSUAWBCZtXbbf2YxxeO%2BcTEiAl2mL6b0%2BoaCMyx2OMwB2q%2BnRCgI2ZPyz0VRw%2FZ4HjDy2kIBSXzWtlgi2421KDQpLMU2BDGfSMmAtWwNriiukqOjkfAoclwURQyYmqu1SGHqg71Lv)

It’s Raul. This isn’t the update I wanted to share today. We fell short on November’s goal, and we need to catch up before the December 31st FEC deadline. As we get closer to the start of the Election Year, it’s critical that we hit every fundraising deadline we set so we’re prepared to take on whatever comes our way. The NRCC have marked me as a top target, and they’ve been spreading lies about me on their media channels. It’s only a matter of time before those attacks begin to flood our airwaves. We’re going to need to build our resources now if we’re to:
* ✔️ air hard-hitting ads that push back against misinformation,
* ✔️ win over new voters in the district before our opponent can,
* ✔️ and build our field operation to energize and mobilize Democratic voters all the way to Election Day.

I know we can do this. Together we can fight back and protect this seat. The son of hardworking farmworkers, Dr. Ruiz grew up in Coachella, CA and learned at an early age that the key to attaining the American Dream is hard work and a great education. In the summer of 1990, Raul walked in the desert heat from business to business in the Coachella Valley asking them to invest in their community by contributing to his education. With each investment for college, he made a promise to come back home and serve his community as a physician. In 2012, he decided to take his service to the community to the next level by running for Congress. **Dr. Ruiz needs our help to continue fighting for us in Congress.**

clean_body: Help us catch up to fight back and protect this seat! While Washington remains gridlocked, Dr. Raul Ruiz is committed to serving and helping constituents with the problems they have with the federal government and cutting red tape. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | It’s Raul. This isn’t the update I wanted to share today. We fell short on November’s goal, and we need to catch up before the December 31st FEC deadline. As we get closer to the start of the Election Year, it’s critical that we hit every fundraising deadline we set so we’re prepared to take on whatever comes our way. The NRCC have marked me as a top target, and they’ve been spreading lies about me on their media channels. It’s only a matter of time before those attacks begin to flood our airwaves. We’re going to need to build our resources now if we’re to: ✔️ air hard-hitting ads that push back against misinformation, ✔️ win over new voters in the district before our opponent can, ✔️ and build our field operation to energize and mobilize Democratic voters all the way to Election Day. I know we can do this. Together we can fight back and protect this seat. = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you for being in this fight with me, Raul Ruiz | | | ---|---|--- = The son of hardworking farmworkers, Dr. Ruiz grew up in Coachella, CA and learned at an early age that the key to attaining the American Dream is hard work and a great education. In the summer of 1990, Raul walked in the desert heat from business to business in the Coachella Valley asking them to invest in their community by contributing to his education. With each investment for college, he made a promise to come back home and serve his community as a physician. In 2012, he decided to take his service to the community to the next level by running for Congress. Dr. Ruiz needs our help to continue fighting for us in Congress. | = = PAID FOR BY DR. RAUL RUIZ FOR CONGRESS COMMITTEE If you would like to unsubscribe, click here: You can also =. Dr. Raul Ruiz for Congress Committee P.O. Box 1566 Indio, CA 92202 United States

Hey folks,
**Katie Phang** is back on _Breaking Bread_ and we covered a lot of emotional ground in this one -- from the chaos of cable news and "platforming" Trump-world figures, to navigating MAGA relatives at Thanksgiving, to Katie taking her kiddo to Anne Frank's house and the D-Day beaches so she understand what democracy really costs. This one gets personal, thoughtful, and honestly pretty tender at times.
Here's what we get into:
* [**00:01:07**] Why we still make content "even if only five people like it," the joy of independent media, and how YouTube/Substack lets us go deep instead of chasing soundbites.
* [**00:04:01**] Katie's take on Abby Phillip having Katie Miller on CNN, the whole "platforming" debate, and what it really means to sit at the table with people who don't share your politics.
* [**00:10:02**] Thanksgiving with MAGA family members, setting boundaries to protect your peace, and what it looks like to decide _not_ to let politics destroy your relationships.
* [**00:20:12**] Katie's trip with her daughter to the Anne Frank House and the D-Day beaches, how those moments imprint on our kids, and why we keep fighting to defend democracy even when we're exhausted.
* [**00:27:03**] Top five American cities, hating winter in Chicago, the heaviness of the holidays, and remembering that not every family moment has to look like a Hallmark movie to be real and meaningful.

If you care about honest, human conversations about politics, family, media, and how we raise our kids in this wild moment, I think this one will land for you. We're trying to model what it looks like to disagree, stay present, and still choose each other -- and your support is what keeps that possible. If you value conversations like this, help me keep them going.

With urgency,
--Mike

name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: Only a few more hours of Giving Tuesday — help us restore the First Amendment
body:
Your gift today will spark a nationwide revival of freedom in 2026.
[](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation/1/0100019ae17edff6-8028bb44-7ab9-4814-9f33-4d0569875875-000000/p1xAXVGGvf6ToXZE5bnCdyqIgP61M_zTBHpPaM63pmY=432)Patriot–**Today is Giving Tuesday,** a day set aside for Americans to come together and defend the freedoms we cherish most. **And this year, as our nation prepares to celebrate 250 years of freedom, your Giving Tuesday gift carries extraordinary significance.** In 2026, Tea Party Patriots Foundation will launch the _America250 Free Speech Revival Bus Tour,_ a nationwide effort to educate, equip, and inspire citizens to use their First Amendment rights of free speech, peaceful assembly, and petitioning the government.From April through June of 2026, we’ll travel to 45 cities across more than two dozen states. [_**Your gift today will determine how many communities we can reach**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation%2Fyearend%2F/1/0100019ae17edff6-8028bb44-7ab9-4814-9f33-4d0569875875-000000/1wcEzJh34r-tZMlbNRaL6ZWFvVXWzXj05j60aq8_7tQ=432)**— and how many Americans will learn what the First Amendment truly protects, how to defend it, and how to use it in their daily lives.** Our tour will be focused on adults because they are the ones who carry the responsibility of passing on our values, our history, and our love of country. If they are not equipped to teach liberty, the next generation cannot defend it, or even know what it is and why they’d want to defend it.**Giving Tuesday is your moment to help ensure America’s 250th birthday is not just a commemoration, but a turning point — a revival of freedom rooted in the First Amendment.**[_**Your tax-deductible gift made _TODAY,_**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation%2Fyearend%2F/2/0100019ae17edff6-8028bb44-7ab9-4814-9f33-4d0569875875-000000/XXrrblDFZIZ_uZNNyPo_RarCMzH3cLP2xQi0T8Il2JU=432)**before midnight tonight,** will help reinvigorate our First Amendment and restore the spirit of liberty for America’s next 250 years and beyond.[MAKE YOUR GIVING TUESDAY GIFT](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation%2Fyearend%2F/3/0100019ae17edff6-8028bb44-7ab9-4814-9f33-4d0569875875-000000/dx6G7djRp3UrYSJh90-H-ZTRDqCkW2-zTRq_YQdZzhg=432)Every hour today matters. Every gift today moves us closer to a national revival of freedom, and I can’t wait to begin this work alongside you.Honored to stand with you for liberty,Jenny Beth Martin**P.S.** You’ve seen the First Amendment weakened and trampled underfoot in recent years. We cannot allow these foundational freedoms — or the understanding of them — to disappear.Please help us _reinvigorate the First Amendment_ and renew the American people’s appreciation for it by [_**giving whatever you can before midnight tonight.**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation%2Fyearend%2F/4/0100019ae17edff6-8028bb44-7ab9-4814-9f33-4d0569875875-000000/dsNTJG9exrUNCAQ7NFSeBfN0ySdYnB2jtiwwQzVN2uY=432) Your gift is**tax-deductible** ,**** and we cannot wait until these freedoms are damaged beyond repair before we act. **Thank you for standing with me in defense of the First Amendment!**[ CONTRIBUTE](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation%2Fyearend%2F/5/0100019ae17edff6-8028bb44-7ab9-4814-9f33-4d0569875875-000000/50DhRYgrca2UzZr_0w9Zbj8mynbEC14jTpakJWRUF5g=432) _This email list is a joint venture of the following separate organizations: Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc., Tea Party Patriots Foundation, and Tea Party Patriots Citizens Fund. All rights reserved.__Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc. operates as a social welfare organization under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code. Contributions to Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc. are not deductible as charitable contributions for income tax purposes._ To donate by mail, send your check made payable to:[_**Tea Party Patriots Foundation**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation%2Fdonate%2F/1/0100019ae17edff6-8028bb44-7ab9-4814-9f33-4d0569875875-000000/iX27AmYYAzVcdRsuL_7-RR6kSanSnDLR2NQs_RZk33w=432)
2295 Towne Lake Pkwy Ste 116-314
Woodstock, GA 30189Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [unsubscribe](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftpot.teapartypatriots.org%2Fcontact-preferences%2F01jp3rk1m05jb3jy5xyw9vqr5t%3Fct=1ebd6a5833cd%26et=a8fe34ad85d9/1/0100019ae17edff6-8028bb44-7ab9-4814-9f33-4d0569875875-000000/qlJG682WjwGY68Y9XHpdHoEmu7goDmOuTxW3Cz-9NwU=432) or [update your preferences](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftpot.teapartypatriots.org%2Fcontact-preferences%2F01jp3rk1m05jb3jy5xyw9vqr5t%3Fct=1ebd6a5833cd%26et=a8fe34ad85d9/2/0100019ae17edff6-8028bb44-7ab9-4814-9f33-4d0569875875-000000/OjKFX5MmlQ41a9rTyVMKyyo9rdd81IQ7CphqoJJRWWk=432).

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. subject: BREAKING: AIPAC is targeting 8 out of 9 Justice Democrats
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Breaking, Peter:

AIPAC launched new ads on Facebook attacking every one of our Justice
Democrats in Congress.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251202.zX8Jaq&refcode=em20251202-37246&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1002&akid=37246%2E1203867%2EyLsKRD ]Collage of ads being run by AIPAC attacking Justice Democrats'
candidates.

Why? For supporting Justice Democrat Congresswoman Rashida Tlaib’s
resolution to formally recognize the Israeli government’s genocide in Gaza
and push the United States to meet its legal obligations to prevent and
punish it.

This is just the beginning of AIPAC’s war on progressive, pro-peace
candidates in the 2026 election cycle. With only one month left in 2025,
they’re no doubt gearing up to drop millions — like they did last cycle —
to try unseating our sitting Justice Democrat members of Congress and stop
our primary challengers against AIPAC’s incumbents.

They’re scared, on their heels, and are desperate to cling to the power
they’re losing because of the movement we’ve built together to reject
their toxic influence in the Democratic Party. Let’s show them why they
should keep being scared.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251202.zX8Jaq&refcode=em20251202-37246&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=37246%2E1203867%2EyLsKRD ]We can’t wait, Peter: we
need your support here and now to show AIPAC we have the resources to
fight back. Will you rush $5 or more now to stand up to
AIPAC?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251202.zX8Jaq&refcode=em20251202-37246&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1022&akid=37246%2E1203867%2EyLsKRD ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

clean_body: [1]Justice Democrats Breaking, Peter: AIPAC launched new ads on Facebook attacking every one of our Justice Democrats in Congress. [ ]Collage of ads being run by AIPAC attacking Justice Democrats' candidates. Why? For supporting Justice Democrat Congresswoman Rashida Tlaib’s resolution to formally recognize the Israeli government’s genocide in Gaza and push the United States to meet its legal obligations to prevent and punish it. This is just the beginning of AIPAC’s war on progressive, pro-peace candidates in the 2026 election cycle. With only one month left in 2025, they’re no doubt gearing up to drop millions — like they did last cycle — to try unseating our sitting Justice Democrat members of Congress and stop our primary challengers against AIPAC’s incumbents. They’re scared, on their heels, and are desperate to cling to the power they’re losing because of the movement we’ve built together to reject their toxic influence in the Democratic Party. Let’s show them why they should keep being scared. [ ]We can’t wait, Peter: we need your support here and now to show AIPAC we have the resources to fight back. Will you rush $5 or more now to stand up to AIPAC? [ ]Contribute $5 In solidarity, Justice Democrats Contribute to Justice Democrats:
